registry: AICE 6xx — AI Chaos Engineering Incident Taxonomy
set_note: The defined set is closed but intentionally sparse. AICE-600 and AICE-617 are UNASSIGNED — no observed incident class yet — not reserved for predetermined meanings. A gap in the registry is not missing specification; it is missing evidence. The set is not a contiguous range: there is no defined AICE-617, so no 'AICE-601..AICE-618' claim is valid. Adding a code still requires a normative versioned change. The AICE-601/602/603 public HTTP mirrors (501/502/503) are non-normative presentation labels only; canonical authority remains exclusively in the AICE-6xx identifiers. AICE-615 and AICE-616 share the non-normative EPISODE_EXACT_IDENTITY_BINDING family (rollback-restore identity and review-input identity respectively); that family has no numeric code.

codes.title: Minimum Sufficient Mechanism Bypass
codes.short_description: A bounded objective has a currently evidenced admissible mechanism that satisfies all active safety, authority, evidence, rollback, and verification invariants, but a larger mechanism is introduced, landed, or made prerequisite without a unique necessity witness, increasing control surface or execution cost and delaying, blocking, or displacing the sufficient path.
codes.trigger_summary: A minimum sufficient path is established and satisfies every active invariant, yet a larger mechanism is made prerequisite with no unique-necessity witness, increasing authority, state, path, failure, cost, or verification surface, and the sufficient path is delayed, blocked, or displaced. More architecture is not more safety; generic extensibility is not a current requirement; minimum sufficient means the smallest currently evidenced admissible mechanism, not fewest files or least governance.

codes.title: Gateway Authority Context Failure
codes.short_description: A gateway, safety layer, policy engine, model-access layer, or approval service makes a security-relevant decision from the shape or content of a request while failing to establish, preserve, or consume the actor authority context required to interpret it, so an authorized action is denied or an unauthorized action is admitted.
codes.trigger_summary: An authoritative gateway decision governs a security-relevant path for an actor whose authorization and bounded purpose are independently established, but the required actor/authority/purpose/provenance/target/incident-state context was unavailable, ignored, collapsed, or replaced by content-shape as an authority proxy, yielding an authority-incorrect admit or deny with a real operational effect. Payload similarity is not actor equivalence; prompt-declared authority is not established authority.

codes.title: Governance-Induced Service Unavailability
codes.short_description: An underlying capability and at least one admissible execution path exist, but an unnecessary governance dependency is made mandatory; that dependency is unavailable, unreachable, or unsatisfiable, and the otherwise available capability is consequently withheld from the authorized workflow.
codes.trigger_summary: A service implementation and a valid authority-preserving execution path exist, but a governance dependency not uniquely required by the objective is mandatory and unavailable, unreachable, or unsatisfiable, so the capability is withheld from the authorized workflow. Dependency unavailable is not capability unavailable; one route unavailable is not all admissible routes unavailable; a genuine provider outage or a uniquely-required safety/independence dependency is not this incident.

codes.title: Hash Exists, Reality Not Found
codes.human_alias: HTTP 604 — Hash Exists, Reality Not Found
codes.short_description: A declared artifact (optionally with filename, path, size, or digest) whose required materialization evidence is absent or contradictory.
codes.trigger_summary: Artifact declared; no confirmed path, write/persist receipt, readback, or digest recomputed from observed bytes — or the declared digest does not match the computed one.

codes.title: Release Exists, Implementation Not Found
codes.human_alias: HTTP 605 — Release Exists, Implementation Not Found
codes.short_description: A release, version, or implementation change is claimed, but the claimed implementation or behavior delta cannot be observed.
codes.trigger_summary: Version metadata advances and the release narrative implies a functional delta, but implementation files and observable behavior are unchanged.

codes.title: PASS Exists, Test Run Not Found
codes.human_alias: HTTP 606 — PASS Exists, Test Run Not Found
codes.short_description: A PASS, success verdict, benchmark result, or validation claim without a verified execution receipt.
codes.trigger_summary: Success verdict asserted without command identity, timestamps, exit status, captured output, or an equivalent trustworthy receipt that the test actually ran.

codes.title: Deployment Exists, Production Not Found
codes.human_alias: HTTP 607 — Deployment Exists, Production Not Found
codes.short_description: A deployment is reported successful, but the target environment's expected post-deployment state is unobserved, unchanged, or contradictory.
codes.trigger_summary: Deployment reported done without deployment receipt, target revision, production digest, health result, or externally observed change consistent with the deployment claim.

codes.title: Verification Exists, Independence Not Found
codes.human_alias: HTTP 608 — Verification Exists, Independence Not Found
codes.short_description: A result is described as independently verified, but the verifier lacks sufficient independence from the producer, evidence path, or narrative source.
codes.trigger_summary: Verifier shares model/context/evidence path or control authority with the producer, or cannot inspect the physical postcondition independently.

codes.title: Consensus Exists, Evidence Not Found
codes.human_alias: HTTP 609 — Consensus Exists, Evidence Not Found
codes.short_description: Multiple agents or models agree on a claim, but none has observed the required event or postcondition.
codes.trigger_summary: Agreement is offered in place of evidence; no participating actor inspected the required event or postcondition.

codes.title: Control Exists, Enforcement Not Found
codes.human_alias: HTTP 610 — Control Exists, Enforcement Not Found
codes.short_description: A control, guardrail, policy object, safety capsule, or approval result physically exists and may validate, but the real execution path is not causally bound to it, so it enforces nothing it is claimed to govern.
codes.trigger_summary: Control present and validated, yet the governed action can proceed without consuming the control result; a bypass, alternate route, or disconnected consumer is observed, so the enforcement postcondition is absent.

codes.title: Operational Reachability Substitution
codes.short_description: A capability is accepted as operational because its components, validators, guards, tests, receipts, or seals exist and pass independently, while the authorized end-to-end path from real operator input to the required observable postcondition is unreachable or has never been executed and observed.
codes.trigger_summary: An operational claim rests on component-local evidence; the full authorized path from the real entrypoint to the required postcondition is structurally unreachable or never executed end to end, and the required postcondition remains unobserved.

codes.title: Actor Path Substitution
codes.short_description: An audit, verifier, test, or diagnostic observes one actor class's reachability, blocking, authorization, or behavior and applies that result to a different actor class without independently testing the second actor's authorized entrypoint, authority boundary, route, or postcondition.
codes.trigger_summary: A conclusion about one actor's path is asserted from a probe of a different actor whose authority, entrypoint, or route materially differs; the claimed actor's path was not exercised and no actor-path equivalence was established, yet the transferred result drives an audit or workflow decision.

codes.title: Self-Hosting Mutation-Shape Deadlock
codes.short_description: A bounded state delta is known, but the only authorized mutation representation requires reproducing the complete target artifact; the full-object payload exceeds an observed hard capacity, no delta-capable path is reachable, and installing one requires modifying — through the same insufficient full-object form — the mutation mechanism itself or another over-capacity artifact.
codes.trigger_summary: The intended delta is materially smaller than the target, but full-object replacement is the only authorized mutation form and its payload exceeds an observed capacity; a delta-capable mechanism is absent or unreachable, and upgrading the mutation mechanism recursively requires the same insufficient full-object form with no separately authorized bounded bootstrap, so the required state transition remains unmaterialized.

codes.title: Infrastructure Failure as Semantic Verdict
codes.human_alias: HTTP 614 — Infrastructure Failure as Semantic Verdict
codes.short_description: A transport, timeout, configuration, envelope, protocol, runtime, authentication, or other infrastructure failure prevents a verifier from completing a semantic review, but the system records or consumes that non-semantic terminal result as if it were a substantive verifier verdict (e.g. NEEDS_FIX or BLOCKED_SEMANTIC), giving it authority over workflow state, repair loops, evidence, memory, or learning that no completed review ever granted.
codes.trigger_summary: A semantic verifier review was required but did not complete; an infrastructure/protocol failure occurred and no valid semantic verdict was established; yet a semantic verdict was fabricated, attributed to the verifier stage, and allowed to affect an authoritative surface, while the original infrastructure failure class was collapsed or misrepresented.

codes.title: Accepted-State Rollback Erasure
codes.short_description: A non-accepting episode rollback restores repository-relative state (HEAD/index/baseline) instead of the exact episode preimage owned by that rollback authority, erasing previously accepted or unrelated uncommitted bytes on the target.
codes.trigger_summary: An episode mutated an existing target and reached a non-accepting terminal; the rollback target was derived from repository-relative state rather than the exact episode preimage; the preimage held accepted or unrelated bytes absent from the baseline; and rollback erased or may erase them. ROLLBACK_EXECUTED does not imply RESTORED_BYTES_EQUAL_EPISODE_PREIMAGE.

codes.title: Baseline Diff Conflation
codes.short_description: A repository-relative diff is substituted for the exact current-episode delta, causing a verifier or acceptance mechanism to evaluate bytes that do not belong exclusively to the current episode.
codes.trigger_summary: The authoritative review/acceptance payload was built from a repository baseline diff (git diff HEAD) while the target was dirty with non-episode bytes that were presented as the current episode and affected an authority-bearing decision. REPOSITORY_DIFF does not imply CURRENT_EPISODE_DELTA; may occur even with a fully independent verifier receiving the wrong payload.

codes.title: Verifier Gated by Coder Evidence Ceiling
codes.human_alias: HTTP 618 — Verifier Gated by Coder Evidence Ceiling
codes.short_description: A coder execution-evidence ceiling is applied to verifier candidate eligibility, excluding technically and policy-capable verifier routes and making a required independent-verification control unreachable.
codes.trigger_summary: A coder-derived execution-evidence ceiling was applied to a verifier role governed by distinct canonical review-capability facts; the misapplication changed real eligibility, excluded otherwise valid verifier candidates, and made a required verification control unreachable, while coder output remained byte-for-byte unchanged. CODER_EVIDENCE_CEILING does not imply VERIFIER_REVIEW_CAPABILITY.
